description:
## Background and intent

ADR-0004 standardizes on TauRPC for the typed Tauri RPC boundary and Effect for frontend async/service code. The user wants upstream untranspiled source available under `docs/research/infra/` as reference material, similar in spirit to the existing Beadwork source reference, while still installing runtime dependencies through pnpm/Cargo in implementation beads.

This is intentionally not traditional vendoring. The reference trees are for reading, debugging, and understanding library behavior. Application code must not import from them. Keeping this as its own bead prevents large research-source diffs from obscuring functional app changes.

## What to build

Add reference-only upstream source checkouts for TauRPC and Effect under `docs/research/infra/`. Use clear directory names, expected to be `docs/research/infra/taurpc/` and `docs/research/infra/effect/`, unless the existing repo conventions strongly point elsewhere.

Record provenance next to the checkouts: upstream URL, exact commit SHA or release tag, date fetched, license note if readily available, and a short statement that app code consumes these libraries through normal package-manager dependencies. Avoid copying generated dependency folders such as `node_modules`, build output, caches, or local editor state. If the upstream repo contains very large generated artifacts, document and exclude them rather than dragging accidental bulk into the project.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] TauRPC source is available under `docs/research/infra/taurpc/` for reference.
- [ ] Effect source is available under `docs/research/infra/effect/` for reference.
- [ ] Adjacent documentation records upstream URL, commit/tag, fetch date, and reference-only intent.
- [ ] The documentation explicitly says application code must install and import TauRPC/Effect through pnpm/Cargo packages, not these reference directories.
- [ ] No application code imports from the reference-source directories.
- [ ] Generated dependency/build/cache folders are excluded from the reference checkouts.
- [ ] The added source/reference material is separated from functional app diffs.

## Out of scope

- Installing TauRPC or Effect into the application.
- Configuring TauRPC, generated types, or Effect services.
- Changing runtime app code.

## Triage

ready-for-agent

comments.text: Planning audit: reference-source checkouts are intentionally separated from functional app work. They are research material only, pinned with provenance, and must not become runtime imports. This keeps large source diffs from obscuring implementation changes while preserving upstream context for future agents.

title: Add TauRPC and Effect reference source checkouts
